skill.name: higgsfield-product-photoshoot
skill.description: Guides Higgsfield product-photoshoot jobs for product images, lifestyle scenes, social ads, hero banners, and visual restyles.
skill.summary: Create product photoshoots and ad-ready visuals with Higgsfield product modes.
skill.author: higgsfield-ai

security_audit.summary: AI review confirms a high-risk remote installation pattern: the skill instructs users to run a GitHub install script through sh. Most static findings for Ruby backticks, weak cryptography, path traversal, and system reconnaissance are false positives caused by Markdown formatting, mode names, placeholder URLs, and interview questions. The skill is not confirmed malicious, but publication should wait until the remote shell install path is removed or replaced with safer installation guidance.

high_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped To Shell
high_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run a GitHub-hosted install script through sh if the Higgsfield CLI is missing. This can execute arbitrary remote code in the user environment and creates a supply-chain risk for a community skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and pipes a remote URL directly to sh. The risk is high even without proof of malicious intent because the executed script is outside the reviewed skill file.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Uploads Local Image Inputs
medium_findings.description: The skill uses a Higgsfield CLI command that sends generation requests to a backend and accepts local image paths that are auto-uploaded. This appears core to the product workflow, but users should understand that product or reference images may leave the local machine.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file states that the backend submits jobs and that local image paths are auto-uploaded. The behavior is legitimate for image generation, but it is a real privacy and data-transfer consideration.

medium_findings.title: Broad Bash Permission For External CLI Workflow
medium_findings.description: The frontmatter allows Bash and the instructions rely on external Higgsfield commands. The commands are documented for the intended workflow, but a marketplace user should review command execution expectations before enabling the skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill clearly declares Bash and uses command examples. I did not find command construction that directly executes untrusted shell fragments, but the permission is still meaningful.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, path traversal, and system reconnaissance findings are not supported by the file context. The relevant lines are Markdown inline code, mode labels, placeholder CDN URLs, or user interview questions, not executable Ruby, cryptographic code, traversal logic, or reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows descriptive Markdown and example output rather than active code. No evidence found for weak cryptography, path traversal, or system reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl To sh Installer Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The install command downloads a shell script from GitHub and executes it immediately. This pattern bypasses normal package verification and should be replaced with pinned, inspectable installation steps.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in a fenced bash block. It is a known dangerous installation pattern regardless of the current contents of the remote script.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Required
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on Higgsfield CLI commands for generation. This is expected for the workflow, but it means the skill cannot operate as plain prompt guidance only.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI dependency is explicit and repeated in examples. I did not find evidence of hidden commands beyond the documented workflow.

content.value_statement: Product teams need polished images for shops, ads, and social channels without rebuilding each prompt by hand. This skill guides Higgsfield product-photoshoot modes and returns ready-to-use image URLs.
actual_capabilities: Selects a Higgsfield product-photoshoot mode from the user request.
Asks short setup questions for count, style, channel, and brand context.
Runs the Higgsfield CLI command for product photoshoot creation.
Supports local product photos or existing upload IDs as references.
Handles studio shots, lifestyle scenes, hero banners, ads, carousels, and restyles.
Returns final image URLs without exposing internal enhanced prompts.
limitations: Requires the Higgsfield CLI, authentication, and backend access.
May upload local product or reference images to Higgsfield services.
Does not generate raw text-to-image results without a product or brand context.
Does not assemble or reveal the final GPT Image 2 prompt.

use_cases.title: Launch Ecommerce Product Images
use_cases.description: Create clean studio or lifestyle images for product pages and marketplace updates.

use_cases.title: Build Paid Social Creative
use_cases.description: Generate coordinated ad variants for Meta, TikTok, Pinterest, or Google campaigns.

use_cases.title: Refresh Brand Visuals
use_cases.description: Restyle existing product shots for seasonal campaigns, website heroes, and social posts.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Studio Product Shot
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one product image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three clean studio product photos for this uploaded bottle. Use a neutral background and make them suitable for Shopify.

prompt_templates.title: Make A Lifestyle Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with channel and mood.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create lifestyle images of my coffee pouch on a sunlit kitchen counter for Instagram. Keep the mood warm, premium, and natural.

prompt_templates.title: Generate An Ad Creative Pack
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced marketing request with offer details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create five paid social ad visuals for this skincare product. Emphasize hydration, a summer launch, and a 20 percent introductory offer.

prompt_templates.title: Restyle Existing Campaign Images
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using an existing image as the subject anchor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Restyle this existing product image into a Christmas campaign version. Preserve the product shape and label, but change the setting and mood.

output_examples.input: Uploaded candle photo with a request for Pinterest visuals.
output: A short question set for count, mood, and placement.
A vertical moodboard pin generation request through Higgsfield.
A concise list of completed image URLs.

output_examples.input: Uploaded drink bottle photo with a request for paid ads.
output: A coordinated ad creative pack with multiple visual variants.
Consistent lighting, product treatment, and campaign direction across the set.
Only the final image links are shown to the user.

output_examples.input: Existing product shot with a request for a holiday refresh.
output_examples.output: A restyled seasonal product image that keeps the product subject while changing the visual context.

best_practices: Upload a clear product photo when fidelity matters.
State the target channel, image count, and campaign goal before generation.
Use restyle mode when the subject must stay the same.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for unrelated text-to-image scenes without a product.
Do not bypass the product-photoshoot command with a direct image model prompt.
Do not upload private product files unless external processing is acceptable.

faq.question: Does this skill require Higgsfield access?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the Higgsfield CLI and requires an authenticated Higgsfield account.

faq.question: Can it work from text only?
faq.answer: It can start from text, but an uploaded product photo gives better product fidelity.

faq.question: What image formats can it create?
faq.answer: It supports product shots, lifestyle scenes, closeups, moodboard pins, hero banners, carousels, ad packs, virtual model tryouts, conceptual images, and restyles.

faq.question: Will it show the enhanced prompt?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to return image URLs without exposing internal prompt assembly.

faq.question: Can it create multiple variants?
faq.answer: Yes. The count option can request multiple distinct images, slides, or ad variants.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential product images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Local image paths may be uploaded to Higgsfield services for processing.
